description: Reusable check families for finding tool and harness changes that clear the adversarially-confirmed to add value bar.
name: Harness Optimization Checks

checks.name: Adjacent tool boundary
checks.prompt_pattern: Ask for a realistic task where two tools look plausible but only one has the correct scope.
checks.promotion_evidence: Baseline selects the adjacent tool and candidate selects the intended tool with no held-out regression.
checks.signal: Wrong next tool when tools overlap, such as scrape versus extract or SQL execution versus migration.

checks.name: No-tool safety
checks.prompt_pattern: Ask for a mutation or unsafe action against a read-only or insufficiently scoped catalog.
checks.promotion_evidence: Candidate selects NO_TOOL or the explicit safe alternative while ordinary read tasks still pass.
checks.signal: Agent calls a tool when the safe answer is to refuse, ask for scope, or use no tool.

checks.name: Argument quality
checks.prompt_pattern: Ask for a task that requires defaults, limits, IDs, filters, response formats, or verification fields.
checks.promotion_evidence: Candidate preserves the right tool choice and improves required argument checks.
checks.signal: Correct tool, wrong or missing arguments.

checks.name: Error recovery
checks.prompt_pattern: Create a first attempt with a likely bad date, ID, permission, or format, then check the retry path.
checks.promotion_evidence: Trace shows the candidate reads the error, fixes the parameter, and avoids repeated invalid calls.
checks.signal: Agent fails to recover from actionable tool errors.

checks.name: Output budget
checks.prompt_pattern: Ask for a broad result where filters, pagination, metadata first, or concise formats should be used.
checks.promotion_evidence: Candidate reduces output size or call count without losing answer accuracy.
checks.signal: Tool output is too large, too raw, or missing context controls.

checks.name: Resource versus tool
checks.prompt_pattern: Ask for information exposed by an MCP resource and a similarly named tool.
checks.promotion_evidence: Candidate uses the resource-first path when it is cheaper or more canonical.
checks.signal: Agent misses MCP resources or overuses tools for static context.

checks.name: Directed thinking
checks.prompt_pattern: Run a task requiring before-first-tool and after-tool reasoning summaries.
checks.promotion_evidence: Trace mentions complexity, tool budget, evidence and stop criteria before tools, then quality, verification, and continue or stop decisions after tools.
checks.signal: Visible reasoning does not plan tool use or verify tool outputs.

checks.name: Harness parity
checks.prompt_pattern: Run the same cases across provider native tools, prompt JSON, SDK loops, IDE agents, and trace adapters.
checks.promotion_evidence: Candidate improves or preserves behavior across harnesses instead of fitting one wrapper.
checks.signal: Same model and tools behave differently across harnesses.

checks.name: Reproducibility
checks.prompt_pattern: Package source pins, matrix hashes, result JSON, exact commands, and examples for every claim.
checks.promotion_evidence: Another maintainer can rerun the matrix from the PR packet and observe the same boundary.
checks.signal: Suggested changes lack source pins, commands, or exact examples.
